item.title: Black man awaiting kidney transplant beaten by hospital security
item.description: The daughter of a late Black kidney patient wants justice against the security guards who attacked and wrongly detained him, according to the St. Louis Post-Dispatch. The shocking incident begins with Hughie Robinson heading back to the Barnes-Jewish Hospital in St. Louis after staff told him he left his wallet there. Just a few days before, the patient, who has stage four renal failure, got the good news: he was second in line for a possible kidney transplant. After retrieving his wallet, Robinson came back to the Kingshighway garage and couldn’t find his Buick. He asked the front desk and even a security guard for help as he continued looking for his vehicle. This reportedly caught the attention of other security guards, who started suspecting he was looking for cars to steal, reporters learned. That’s when the guards start approaching Robinson and forcefully grabbed his arm, which was tender from a procedure, according to a lawsuit filed last year against Barnes-Jewish. The then 52-year-old man allegedly asked them, ‘What did I do?’ before one of the guards tackled him, the filing claims. Then security allegedly started beating him up — including the guard tasked with helping Robinson search for his Buick. “Hughie cried out that the guards were hurting him. At least one of the guards responded, ‘Good.’ The guards then forced Hughie into a pair of handcuffs. All of this was on video,” the lawsuit alleges. The document says Robinson was taken to an interrogation room, where hospital security asked him what he was doing in the garage. The lawsuit also claims an officer used “his forearm to smash Hughie’s head into the wall” while another threatened the kidney patient. “If one of my officers, or WashU officers see you back on this property again, we’re going to do this whole thing again, cause you ain’t supposed to be here. You cool with that? … Don’t come back.” Robinson just finished a four-day stay at the hospital when the vicious assault happened, according to reporters, adding that he was drugged and “in a weakened state” when security allegedly beat him up. That following June, Robinson filed a lawsuit against the hospital for assault, battery, and false imprisonment, but he later died from his illness. Now his daughter, Chelsea, is seeking justice on her late father’s behalf. “It’s important for me to continue the case because my dad deserves to be at peace, and it’s what he would have wanted … his words exactly,” she told the Atlanta Black Star. “So, it’s only my duty to do right by his name.” She finally got to see the surveillance video after St. Louis Circuit Court Judge Joan Moriarity reversed a protective order that kept the footage from getting released. She says watching the video left her heartbroken. “He was just standing there, not doing anything,” the daughter told reporters. Source: https://newpittsburghcourier.com/2022/07/17/black-man-awaiting-kidney-transplant-beaten-by-hospital-security/
item.dc:creator: Igotfourbedrats

item.title: One more reason to NOT shop at Walgreens
item.description: “I said, ‘You won’t refill it, or you can’t refill it?’” Martin said. Martin called her doctor, who told her she had four refills on the prescription. The doctor’s office called Walgreens. Then Martin received two more texts, one saying the medicine was delayed and another alleging it was “out of stock.” These people should fired! What would they do if someone refused to sell them their medication?

item.title: Six kids and two adults requires a very large picnic table, so I made a Very Large Picnic Table with eight seats and a lazy susan for outdoor dining.
item.description:
Obligatory finished product first. From the furthest out points, it's about 8'6" across, and the tabletop is about 6' across. The lazy susan (which did not survive the weather and bowed like mad) is 3' across. It is supported with a 4" bearing in the middle and smaller ball casters at four points to support the edges when laden with barbecue foods. I'll be replacing it with a concrete version eventually.
Step one, get hella lumber. This is all kiln dried douglas fir, due to its relatively high strength by weight. Good stuff.
I built a frame that looked a lot like a sailing ship's helm wheel, and screwed it into the top decking from the back, making all the fasteners hidden.
Once I got all of the boards screwed in, I marked out and cut the octagon. I ended up having to trim it all back to make it more even, not sure where I miscalculated, but there were some adjustments made.
After cutting the top to size and shape, i added the edge boards to make it comfortable to sit at.
All rimmed up. I used a 2" forstner bit to drill a hole all the way through the center, to fit an umbrella.
I was considering moving it myself, but it weighed about 400 lbs. I could have rolled it, but that would have chewed up the edges so bad.
So instead I built the bench seats and the base, making sure it was all good and strong and secure. We carried it into place and fetched the top.
I made this poor lady help me carry it around the side of the house. Holding it over our heads to get it over the 6' tall fence was... not easy. But we got it in place, and it is rock solid!
Time to start sealing it. I used a redwood stain, as I had originally wanted to use redwood decking for the top, but that was WAY too expensive (about 4x the price).
The difference is dramatic, and I -really- like the color. Sheds water really well too.
And all done with the stain!
It's missing something.... It needs a lazy susan turntable to eb able to have a decent barbecue without passing plates and whatever all over the place. Don't mind if I do!
Edge glued the cutoff pieces into an appropriately size piece and made a makeshift circle jig.
First pass. I could only go maybe 3/8" deep at a time, and that was pushing it.
That is all the way through, btu the edges were super rough. Time for the roundover bit.
Ah, much better. I epoxied some super thin (0.05") plexiglass to the bottom for the ball casters to ride on, so they wouldn't wear a groove in the bottom under weight, and drilled a hole to allow the installation of the central bearing.
Worked like a charm! until weather made it bow because I didn't get to sealing the top soon enough... We're going to make a 3' round form with a 2" plug in the middle that I will pour a new disk into, then have the kids either press glass beads into the wet concrete, or their handprints and ages at the time. Then I'll seal it with some concrete sealer and reinstall it where the ruined wooden one is. THAT oughtta last. Thanks for reading! Most Viral Edit: I've had a few of y'all ask me for the plans, so here's a link to a sketchup file that has the design I used, to scale, with all the appropriate angles and everything, including a separated duplicate tabletop so you can see how the underpinnings were cut. It is a Sketchup .skp file, so you'll need Sketchup to view it. i used Sketchup 2017, from when it was still free to download and use privately. https://drive.google.com/file/d/19fIlo-iT5o86UWl7TZLAWKVCrWDPKV85/view?usp=sharing

item.title: Lil dump-a-rooni
item.description:
1. A coworker was shot in a drive-by (collateral damage). They replaced her 2 weeks later.
2. Something has to fix this timeline. Not enough mutual aid for everyone to protest en masse.
3. Ah, but which bean? Bring me a freshly harvested Terrazu.
4. Went and danced in a microburst this week. Don’t forget to water yourself.
5. The bees and flutterbys will thank you.
6. If you say fast enough, guests won’t know either. Well, some might.
7. I need this as a gift for someone.
8. This works, btw.
9.
10.
11.
12.
13.
14. There’s an inordinate number of dog sweaters for some reason.
15. Or this is you because of a crazy day. It’s ok to take a mental health day. Self care is important.
16. Van Webb
17. And you’ve got a great butt
18.
19. The cicadas are full tilt right now.
20. Is it the same dog?
21. It was a simple rule.
22. For those deep reflection highs
23. One of my dogs legit chuffs. He knows.
24. What spells today?
24. Goth Muppets?
25. I would not have lasted 2 weeks.
26. Faster anxiety is one way to clean your house.
27. Such a dark crossover.
28. Aaaah, that’s the good stuff.
29. There’s no need for this now. It isn’t the summer of ‘82.
30. AZ summer. They shoild seriously give you a pair when you buy a car here.
31. Fight until the very end.
32. Stop trying to juggle. Take a break. Enjoy time with friends or family. There’s always another day for the list of things. Time with hoomans is limited.
33. Mycology is a fascinating science.
34. Couldn’t hurt.
35.
36.
37. Or at a friend’s. Or community pool.
38.
39.
40. You can visibly see it.
41.
42.
43. May none of them have been on the top shelf.
44.
45. Kodo tax
46. Sangha tax

item.dc:creator: huntsnhava40

item.title: Hallelujah!
item.description: Was initially super excited to learn from him and his experience until his true nature was revealed. Then I couldn't learn from him let alone respect him once I got to see what kind of a person he really was. From day one he happily bragged about "liberating" all of his files from his previous employer. Talked down to everyone, except those he saw as worthy of his respect. Told the whole team he wasn't going to be around much while his family was in town, but didn't book it off because he was "saving it for later" but wanted micro managed constantly because he wanted to make sure we were at our most productive. Badmouthed his direct reports to other direct reports. Never gave an ounce of praise. Bragged about being amazing at getting what he wanted by manipulating others. Would show up late to team meetings then hijack like it was his time to soapbox then with the last 5 minutes would ask if anyone else had anything. Yelled at certain people because they were behind on his out-to-lunch deadlines. But he was smart, practiced and that's what made it worse. Some thought he was charming and really good at his job because he was very careful about never letting it slip in text or places it could be corroborated. Only in private meetings via video. On paper he was golden. Found out his last employment threw a party when he left and got this job. An absolutely dogshit human. Went to HR and we supported eachother in speaking out against him. Recorded some of the manipulating and few degrading things he let slip by. Luckily I work for a great (huge) company, one I wasn't sure would go to these lengths (they put perceived massive business targets at risk to fire him) and was fully ready to leave or get fired myself. I both wanted to celebrate into the void and post to hopefully encourage others to not tolerate this bs from absolute trash fire humans. I was so excited i had to make an alt. Life is way too short to be treated like garbage to be used and discarded. We all deserve better. EDIT: I realize it is more of a success kid but I did feel kinda bad he doesn't know I was pushing for his removal from the sidelines. Also I'm sorry so many others are dealing with remorseless psychos like this.
item.dc:creator: sometimesyoujustcantgetridofabomb
